body.Headquarters: Foster City / San Francisco, California, USA
body.Industry: AI-Powered Software Development & Agentic Application Platform (Cloud IDE / Vibe Coding)
body.LatestValuation: $9 billion (March 2026, Series D led by Georgian)
body.TotalFundingRaised: ~$878M–$922M across multiple rounds (PitchBook cites $878M; Latka cites $871.5M; Sacra estimates $650M+ across last two primary rounds alone, with earlier rounds adding ~$222M+)
body.FundingRounds: Pre-Seed: $120K (Y Combinator, 2018) | Seed: $4.5M (Andreessen Horowitz) | Series A: $20M (A.Capital Ventures) | Series B: $80M (Coatue, 2021) | Series C: $97.4M (Andreessen Horowitz, April 2023, valuation ~$1.16B) | Venture Round: $20M (Craft Ventures) | Series E / Round: $250M (Prysm Capital, September 10, 2025, valuation ~$3B) | Series D: $400M (Georgian, March 2026, valuation $9B)
body.KeyInvestors: Georgian (Series D lead), Prysm Capital (Series E lead), Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), Coatue Management, Google AI Futures Fund, Amex Ventures (American Express), Y Combinator, Craft Ventures, Khosla Ventures, A.Capital Ventures, G Squared, Accenture Ventures, Okta Ventures, Databricks Ventures, Bloomberg Beta, Paul Graham, SV Angel
body.EmployeeCountEstimate: ~170–282 (PitchBook: 170; Latka: 282 as of 2026; Summit Ventures: ~150–200 in late 2025; Growjo: ~205)
body.RevenueEstimate: $240M–$300M (2025 reported revenue); ~$150M ARR as of September 2025; targeting $1B ARR by end of 2026. Prior baseline: ~$2.8M ARR in early 2024, growing 50x+ in under one year post-AI Agent launch
body.GrowthMetrics: ARR grew from ~$2.8M (early 2024) to ~$150M (September 2025) — a 50x+ increase in under one year; valuation tripled from $3B to $9B in ~6 months (Sept 2025 to March 2026); 40M–50M+ registered users globally; 150K–175K paying customers; 500K+ business/professional users; 85% of Fortune 500 reportedly have users on platform; revenue per employee estimated at over $1M; $240M in 2025 full-year revenue with $1B target for 2026
body.RecentDealsAndExits: Acquisition of OpenInt (closed September 30, 2025, per PitchBook); Products listed on Google Cloud Marketplace (announced September 2025); Microsoft Azure Marketplace integration; Partnership with Hexaware for enterprise vibe coding (August 2025); Government of Jordan partnership (Siraj AI learning assistant, 100K+ students/teachers); CEO Amjad Masad reportedly declined a ~$1B acquisition offer from GitHub
body.ComparableCompanies: Cursor (Anysphere) — $29.3B valuation, $500M ARR; Lovable — $6.6B valuation; Windsurf (acquired by OpenAI for ~$3B); GitHub Copilot (Microsoft); StackBlitz ($80M raised); Gitpod ($41M raised); CodeSandbox; Vercel; Bubble; Glitch; Codeanywhere; GitHub Codespaces
body.DataConfidenceNotes: Replit is a private company and does not publicly disclose audited financials. Revenue and ARR figures are based on company statements, CEO announcements, and third-party estimates (Reuters, Forbes, Sacra, SaaStr, Latka) and may differ from actual internal figures. Employee counts vary across sources (170–282) due to differing data collection dates. Funding totals vary slightly by source ($871M–$922M) depending on whether all secondary/venture rounds are included. Valuation reflects most recent primary round (March 2026 Series D at $9B). All data should be treated as estimates for private market research purposes.
